title: Contract Tests, Race and Leak Checks, Chaos, and Release · Runtime Contract: Production Defense
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must layer pure tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable-broker faults, compatible rollout, upgrade, rollback, and recovery proof; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the production defense increment in a production Go messaging defense dossier. Learners…
objectives: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 upgrade on supported 4.2 path, Erlang compatibility, feature state, alarms, replica health, client tests, rolling sequence, and rollback limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Production Defense · Theory Production Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 1603 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on contracts, race and leak evidence, broker faults, compatibility, rollback, and upgrade path; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Production Defense contract for the supplied data.
